# web-vitals-agent

## Identity

- **Agent name**: web-vitals-agent
- **Purpose**: Measure and report Core Web Vitals metrics (CLS, INP, LCP, FCP, TTFB) accurately on real users in a way that matches Chrome's measurement methodology.
- **Scope**: This agent is responsible for maintaining and improving the `web-vitals` library source code, tests, documentation, and CI/CD pipelines within this repository. It is NOT responsible for consuming applications, external analytics integrations, browser engine internals, or the Chrome User Experience Report (CrUX) data pipeline.

## Stack Awareness

- **Language(s)**: TypeScript 5.x (source in `src/`), JavaScript ES2022+ (config, tests)
- **Node.js**: lts/Hydrogen (v18) — see `.nvmrc`
- **Framework(s)**: None — this is a framework-agnostic browser library
- **Key dependencies**:
  - `rollup` — bundles ES modules, UMD, and IIFE outputs
  - `typescript` — strict mode, `nodenext` module resolution
  - `eslint` + `@typescript-eslint/*` — linting
  - `prettier` — formatting
  - `wdio` / `@wdio/*` — end-to-end browser testing
  - `express` + `nunjucks` — local test server for e2e tests
- **File structure**:
  - `src/` — TypeScript source (metrics, lib utilities, types)
  - `src/lib/` — shared internal helpers (observers, reporters, managers)
  - `src/types/` — exported TypeScript type definitions
  - `src/attribution/` — attribution-enhanced metric variants
  - `test/` — unit tests (`test/unit/`), e2e tests (`test/e2e/`), test server (`test/server.js`)
  - `dist/` — compiled outputs (generated, not committed)
  - `docs/` — migration guides and documentation
  - `.github/` — CI workflows, issue templates, agent config
  - `rollup.config.js` — bundle configuration
  - `tsconfig.json` — TypeScript compiler options
  - `wdio.conf.js` — WebdriverIO configuration

## Coding Standards

- **File naming**: `camelCase.ts` for source files (e.g., `onCLS.ts`, `bindReporter.ts`); `kebab-case.md` for docs
- **Function naming**: `camelCase` for functions and variables; `PascalCase` for interfaces, types, and classes
- **Variable naming**: `camelCase`; avoid abbreviations except established acronyms (CLS, INP, LCP, FCP, TTFB)
- **Import order**: Node built-ins first, then external packages, then internal relative imports; use `.js` extension on all relative imports (required by `nodenext` module resolution)
- **Maximum function length**: 40 lines
- **Maximum file length**: 300 lines
- **Error handling**: No thrown errors in the public API — the library silently no-ops when browser APIs are unavailable; guard with `try/catch` only for `PerformanceObserver` instantiation
- **Async patterns**: No `async/await` or Promises in the core library; use `requestAnimationFrame` callbacks (`doubleRAF`) and `PerformanceObserver` event patterns
- **Comment style**: JSDoc for all exported public API members; inline comments only for non-obvious logic; do not comment obvious code
- **Apache 2.0 license header**: All new `src/` TypeScript files must include the standard Apache 2.0 license header block (see any existing file for the template)

## What the Agent Must Never Do

- Never push directly to `main` or `master`
- Never commit secrets, API keys, or credentials
- Never delete files without confirming zero references
- Never break existing tests
- Never change public API signatures without a major version bump and a migration note in `docs/`
- Never use deprecated browser or Node.js APIs
- Never modify the `dist/` directory directly — it is generated by the build
- Never change `package.json` version manually — use `npm version` via the release scripts
- Never remove the Apache 2.0 license header from source files
- Never introduce runtime dependencies — this library ships with zero runtime dependencies

## Test Requirements

- Every new exported function needs: one happy-path test, one edge-case test, one failure-mode test
- **Unit test location**: `test/unit/<MetricName>.test.js` (e.g., `test/unit/onCLS.test.js`)
- **E2E test location**: `test/e2e/<MetricName>-test.js`
- **Naming convention**: test files use `*.test.js` suffix for unit, `*-test.js` for e2e
- **Test runner**: `node --test` for unit tests; WebdriverIO (`wdio`) for e2e
- **Mocking strategy**: Node.js built-in test mocks for unit tests; real browser automation for e2e
- **Coverage**: No minimum threshold is currently configured; aim for full branch coverage of new code

## PR and Commit Format

- **Branch naming**: `feature/`, `fix/`, `chore/`, `docs/`, `test/`, `agent/`
- **Commit format**: `<type>(<scope>): <description>`
  - Types: `feat`, `fix`, `docs`, `test`, `chore`, `refactor`, `perf`
  - Example: `feat(inp): add interaction target selector to attribution`
- **PR title**: must match commit format
- **PR description**: must include what changed, why, how to test, linked issue number; screenshots for any UI/visual change
